note: FROZEN Stage 1 manifest (version 1.2.1), approved. Reviewed across four revisions; see BENCHMARK_CONTRACT.md (approved Stage 1 contract) for the full rationale. This file's content must not change without a new manifest_version. manifest_sha256 is computed over this file's canonical JSON serialization by ingestion_bench.canonical.hashing.compute_manifest_sha256 and is intentionally not embedded in this file (a hash of the manifest cannot include itself).
domain: Enterprise Resilience (synthetic, unrelated to the src/ GraphRAG POC corpus)

parity_suite.description: Same semantic knowledge rendered natively in DOCX, PDF, and PPTX. TWO logical units per format: unit 0 = text/distractors/table, unit 1 = picture/caption. A hard unit boundary (page break in DOCX/PDF, slide break in PPTX) is inserted between them.

headings.text: Enterprise Resilience Overview: Payment Settlement Service

paragraphs.text: Application APP-224510 supports the Payment Settlement business service.
APP-224510 is in scope for Regulatory Obligation O-31.
Regulatory Obligation O-31 is satisfied by Control C-88.
Control C-88 mandates Recovery Procedure P-205 for critical payment-processing applications.
Payment Settlement is owned by the Resilience Engineering Team and operates in EMEA.

identifiers.note: Occurrence lists below are the CURATED, formally tracked ground truth used by the identifier-recall metrics. They are deliberately not an exhaustive substring scan: distractor_facts D_001 and D_002 each also mention the real identifier once in passing (APP-224510 in D_001's 'superseded by' clause; C-88 in D_002's 'replaced by' clause) for narrative closure — these incidental mentions are intentionally NOT double-counted here, to keep the tracked ground truth curated rather than derived by blind text-scanning. If occurrence-level recall should instead be scored as an exhaustive scan, that is an open item for Stage 2 (see BENCHMARK_CONTRACT.md).

occurrences.note: OCR/vision-visible inside the parity image itself, not plain text

distractor_identifiers.false_merge_risk: Shares a 6-digit numeric prefix pattern with APP-224510 but is a distinct token. A parser must not normalize/merge it into APP-224510.

Contains 'C-88' as a literal substring. Matching MUST use exact identifier token boundaries (the character immediately following '88' must not be alphanumeric) so 'C-88' is never counted as occurring inside 'C-88a', and vice versa 'C-88a' must never be counted as a match for the real identifier 'C-88'.

distractor_facts.text: APP-224499 was the predecessor system for payment settlement and was decommissioned in 2024; it is superseded by APP-224510.
distractor_facts.purpose: Confusable identifier (APP-224499 vs. APP-224510), near-identical prefix — tests whether extraction distinguishes them instead of merging/aliasing.

distractor_facts.text: Legacy Control C-88a was retired and replaced by Control C-88; references to C-88a in older documents are historical only.
distractor_facts.purpose: Confusable identifier (C-88a vs. C-88), C-88 is a literal substring of C-88a — the primary token-boundary stress case.

tables.notes: Row 3 is a distractor row tied to the retired C-88a identifier. Native table (DOCX table / PDF TableFormer / PPTX native table) — never routed through vision enrichment.

captions.text: Figure 1: Escalation flow from incident detection to activation of Recovery Procedure P-205.

pictures.description_for_generator: One deterministically-generated PNG (via Pillow, fixed coordinates, no randomness), embedded identically in all three formats. Canvas 700x160px, white background.
pictures.expected_picture_class_note: Provisional label. Must be reconciled against Docling's actual picture-classifier label taxonomy during Stage 5 implementation.

parity_suite.diagram_nodes_edges_note: These are recoverable from the flat parity image ONLY via a VisionEnricher (paths B/C/D) mapping to DiagramNodeAnnotation/DiagramEdgeAnnotation with extraction_method identifying the enricher. DOCLING_STANDARD_LOCAL (path A) alone cannot recover node/edge semantics from a flat raster image — it may recover the OCR text tokens only (see expected_ocr_tokens above), via a separate OcrAnnotation.

visual_distractor_facts.claim: The escalation flow includes a fourth step for executive notification.
visual_distractor_facts.purpose: Plausible-sounding but false node claim. Scored only against structured DiagramNodeAnnotation/DiagramEdgeAnnotation output — never against ImageDescriptionAnnotation/summary prose, which is human-review only and is never deterministically scored.

stress_suite.description: Small, format-specific and capability-specific fixtures. No cross-format parity required.

docx_nested_structure.notes: Tests multi-level heading hierarchy (H1>H2>H3) and 3-level nested list fidelity, native DOCX structures.

paragraphs.text: Column one covers the primary recovery timeline for Payment Settlement.
Column two covers the secondary failover timeline for the same service.

tables.notes: Header row has a col_span=2 merged cell; tests row/column fidelity under merged cells. Native TableFormer extraction — never vision-enriched.

pdf_complex_layout.notes: Tests reading order under a two-column layout plus a merged-cell table, entirely within DOCLING_STANDARD_LOCAL (no VLM).

text_boxes.text: Superseded annotation: RTO target 6h (draft, do not use)

pptx_overlapping_textboxes.evaluation_expectation: BOTH SB_001 and SB_002 must be extracted as distinct blocks. z_order must be retained on each block where the format/parser exposes it. The parser/evaluator must NOT infer that front-most (higher z_order) implies authoritative or correct — both are equally valid extracted facts; picking a 'winner' is out of scope.
pptx_overlapping_textboxes.notes: Native PPTX table alongside two overlapping text boxes (one stale, rendered behind).

pptx_native_diagram.description: Native PPTX shapes + connectors (not a rasterized image). This fixture measures an EXPLORATORY capability, not an assumed one: whether DOCLING_STANDARD_LOCAL can reconstruct diagram structure from native shape objects. No capability is assumed in advance.

capability_breakdown.node_labels: Can the text labels of each shape be extracted at all (independent of shape/connector detection)?
capability_breakdown.shapes: Can the shape objects themselves be detected as discrete elements (bbox, count)?
capability_breakdown.connectors: Can connector objects between shapes be detected as discrete elements, independent of knowing what they connect?
capability_breakdown.source_target_linkage: For a detected connector, can its source shape and target shape be correctly identified?
capability_breakdown.direction: For a detected connector, can its directionality (arrow direction) be correctly identified?

chart_visual_stress.description: One deterministically-generated bar chart (Pillow, fixed coordinates, no randomness) — quarterly recovery-test pass rates. For vision-enricher scoring (paths B/C/D) only; DOCLING_STANDARD_LOCAL alone has no semantic-numeric-reading capability.

unsupported_claims.subject: Q2 pass rate
unsupported_claims.reason: Q2 value is 88%, not >95%. A correct VisualFactAnnotation set must NOT assert this. Scored only against structured VisualFactAnnotation output, never against summary prose.

scanned_pdf_ocr_stress.description: Single-page PDF containing ONLY a rasterized image of text (no digital text layer at all) — forces OCR to be the sole extraction path. Contrasts with the born-digital parity PDF. This is real OcrAnnotation territory (RapidOCR / Docling's OCR pipeline), not a VisionEnricher/VisibleTextAnnotation case.
scanned_pdf_ocr_stress.expected_ocr_text: This page intentionally contains no digital text layer. Recovery Procedure P-205 must be tested quarterly.
